[[BLOCK:p_79]]
Academic Integrity Policy:

[[BLOCK:p_81]]
Hawaii Pacific University (HPU) is committed to fostering a learning environment grounded not only in academic excellence but also in academic integrity. In this course, you are expected to comply with all HPU policies regarding academic dishonesty. University policy stipulates that any act of academic dishonesty will result in disciplinary action, up to and including dismissal from the university. Students who cheat on academic work, provide unauthorized assistance to others, or submit work that is not their own will be subject to sanctions. Academic work includes all assignments submitted for credit. For the complete policy, please refer to the Student Handbook: www.hpu.edu/studenthandbook

[[BLOCK:p_83]]
Accessibility Services (Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA)):

[[BLOCK:p_85]]
In accordance with Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 and Title III of the ADA Amendments Act of 2008 (ADAAA) (ADAAA), which addresses public accommodations, students who believe they may need accommodations due to a disability are encouraged to contact the Office of Accessibility Services at HPU by phone at (808) 544-1197, by email at access@hpu.edu, or in person at Waterfront Plaza, 500 Ala Moana Blvd, Tower 6 Suite 440, Honolulu, HI 96813.

[[BLOCK:p_87]]
Contacting Accessibility Services is a necessary step to ensure that reasonable accommodations are provided for this course. Students are not required to disclose specific details of their disability to the professor; Accessibility Services will provide faculty with a letter outlining the approved accommodations. If you would like to discuss other concerns, such as potential medical emergencies or assistance needed during an emergency evacuation, please schedule a meeting with the professor as soon as possible.

[[BLOCK:p_89]]
Counseling and Behavioral Health Services (CBHS) (CBHS)

[[BLOCK:p_91]]
Counseling and Behavioral Health Services (CBHS) offers free, confidential counseling to currently enrolled HPU students. At times, academic pressures and personal challenges can feel overwhelming. CBHS provides students with a supportive space to discuss personal concerns and explore possible solutions. To schedule an appointment, call (808) 687-7076 or visit the Waterfront Plaza campus at 500 Ala Moana Blvd., Tower 6, Suite 402, Honolulu, HI 96813. Office hours are Monday through Friday, 9:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.

[[BLOCK:p_93]]
HPU Libraries and Learning Commons

[[BLOCK:p_95]]
The HPU Libraries provide services and resources to support the research needs of the HPU community. The Waterfront Library (WP6-302) and the Atherton Library at the Hawaiʻi Loa campus offer print books, journals, and audiovisual materials. Online resources—including articles, e-books, and streaming video—are available through the library website at hpu.edu/libraries. When accessing online content off campus, users will be prompted to log in through my.hpu.edu authentication. Students may seek research assistance at the reference desk at any library location. Librarians are also available by phone (808.544.1133), by email (reference@hpu.edu), and through the library’s 24/7/365 online chat service (hpu.on.worldcat.org/chat/librarian). To schedule a research consultation with a subject specialist, visit hpu.libcal.com/appointments.

[[BLOCK:p_97]]
Military and Veterans Center (MVC)

[[BLOCK:p_99]]
The Military and Veterans Center has a part-time mental health provider from the Department of Veterans Affairs (退伍军人管理局) who offers confidential counseling services to veterans. Transitioning from military to civilian life, adjusting to academic demands, and managing other personal challenges can at times be difficult. An on-site provider is available at the Downtown campus, and appointments can be scheduled by calling (808) 940-4287. The office is located at 900 Fort Street Mall, Suite 200, Honolulu, HI 96813. Office hours are Monday, Tuesday, and most Fridays from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m.
